issue.title: sandbox.co
issue.body:
**Issue type:** Incorrectly blocked domain (false positive in AdGuard DNS server-side filtering)

**Domains affected:** `sandbox.co` and `api.sandbox.co` (whole-domain block)

**What happens:** AdGuard public DNS sinkholes both hosts to the block page on the Default AND Family Protection servers, while the Non-filtering server resolves them correctly:

```
$ dig sandbox.co @94.140.14.14 +short    # AdGuard DNS Default
94.140.14.33
$ dig sandbox.co @94.140.14.140 +short   # AdGuard non-filtering
172.67.208.122
104.21.91.22

$ dig api.sandbox.co @94.140.14.14 +short
94.140.14.33
$ dig api.sandbox.co @94.140.14.140 +short
104.21.91.22
172.67.208.122
```
(94.140.14.33 = ad-block.dns.adguard.com block page; captured 2026-07-07)

**Why this is a false positive:** sandbox.co hosts **Sandbox**, a legitimate B2B SaaS platform (go-to-market workspaces), served via Cloudflare. `api.sandbox.co` is its REST API — blocking it breaks the application entirely for AdGuard DNS users. The domain appears in **none** of the public filter lists AdGuard aggregates (checked the AdGuardSDNSFilter build and all Hostlists Registry lists, plus oisd/HaGeZi/StevenBlack/EasyList/URLhaus/OpenPhish — zero exact-domain hits), so the block appears to come from AdGuard's server-side dangerous/parked-domains database.

The likely cause is **stale reputation**: the domain was a parked ad page for years (~2019-2025) and was acquired and relaunched as this product in early 2026. McAfee TrustedSource had the same stale "Parked Domain" classification; recategorization requests have been filed there as well. Google Safe Browsing and Norton SafeWeb both rate the domain clean, and the former wildcard DNS record is gone.

**Request:** please remove `sandbox.co` (and subdomains) from the DNS filtering database or recategorize it as a legitimate business/SaaS domain.

(Attempted to file via reports.adguard.com but the screenshot upload step repeatedly failed with "Something went wrong", so filing directly — the dig transcript above is the full repro.)

- Updated watchers.yml with new last_known_mirror values

## Verification
Please review the changes before merging.

This happens about every one hour or so and it’s something that AdGuard has not yet blocked. This happened after MangaFire got a maintenance update, and I first removed my history then I updated my AdGuard to make sure ads or redirects appeared, but it seems AdGuard has missed one. > > Please give AdGuard an update to fix this issue, and let me know when the update is done by emailing me. issue.title: sandbox.co
issue.body:
**Issue type:** Incorrectly blocked domain (false positive in AdGuard DNS server-side filtering)

**Domains affected:** `sandbox.co` and `api.sandbox.co` (whole-domain block)

**What happens:** AdGuard public DNS sinkholes both hosts to the block page on the Default AND Family Protection servers, while the Non-filtering server resolves them correctly:

```
$ dig sandbox.co @94.140.14.14 +short    # AdGuard DNS Default
94.140.14.33
$ dig sandbox.co @94.140.14.140 +short   # AdGuard non-filtering
172.67.208.122
104.21.91.22

$ dig api.sandbox.co @94.140.14.14 +short
94.140.14.33
$ dig api.sandbox.co @94.140.14.140 +short
104.21.91.22
172.67.208.122
```
(94.140.14.33 = ad-block.dns.adguard.com block page; captured 2026-07-07)

**Why this is a false positive:** sandbox.co hosts **Sandbox**, a legitimate B2B SaaS platform (go-to-market workspaces), served via Cloudflare. `api.sandbox.co` is its REST API — blocking it breaks the application entirely for AdGuard DNS users. The domain appears in **none** of the public filter lists AdGuard aggregates (checked the AdGuardSDNSFilter build and all Hostlists Registry lists, plus oisd/HaGeZi/StevenBlack/EasyList/URLhaus/OpenPhish — zero exact-domain hits), so the block appears to come from AdGuard's server-side dangerous/parked-domains database.

The likely cause is **stale reputation**: the domain was a parked ad page for years (~2019-2025) and was acquired and relaunched as this product in early 2026. McAfee TrustedSource had the same stale "Parked Domain" classification; recategorization requests have been filed there as well. Google Safe Browsing and Norton SafeWeb both rate the domain clean, and the former wildcard DNS record is gone.

**Request:** please remove `sandbox.co` (and subdomains) from the DNS filtering database or recategorize it as a legitimate business/SaaS domain.

(Attempted to file via reports.adguard.com but the screenshot upload step repeatedly failed with "Something went wrong", so filing directly — the dig transcript above is the full repro.)

comment.body: Unblocked.

issue.title: www.adobe.com

labels.name: T: Incorrect Blocking
labels.description: Website or app not working properly due to filtering

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking)
[https://www.adobe.com/home](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.adobe.com%2Fhome)

### Comment
> Could you please remove from your default DNS rules, whether your DNS in IPv4 and IPv6, your DoH, DoT, DoQ and DNSCrypt IPv4 and IPv6 the following one: ||cchome.adobe.io^, as it breaks the webpage https://www.adobe.com/home for Creative Cloud Pro users. Please refer to the screenshot attached to this message to see what happens by enforcing this rule. I hope you'll answer positively to this request because it is really a pain in the neck to have to choose between your DNS filtering rules and the expected display of a website. Hope you'll understand, and remove this.



### Screenshots
<details>
  <summary>Screenshot 1</summary>

  ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/izb1murw1qg4g8k888cwc400gwckkkckzeo6varshs.png?nc=1)
</details>

<details>
  <summary>Screenshot 1 with AdGuard disabled</summary>

  ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/pu66va7rb28wcwc888ok8sg4gsws4cccwlk6s91acg.png?nc=1)
</details>

### System configuration

| Information | Value |
|-------------|-------|
AdGuard product: | AdGuard DNS
DNS setup: | Other
DNS server: | Default
System version: | `Windows 11, Version 25H2 (build du système d'exploitation 26200.8037)`
Browser: | Firefox

comment.body:
[adobe_dns_bypass.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29793530/adobe_dns_bypass.zip)
Man, that cchome rule is a total nightmare for CC users. I ended up tweaking the local resolver config to bypass that specific domain block so the assets actually load right. It worked like a charm for me.
